answer:
\boxed{A}

The **bid-rent theory** suggests that wealthier individuals or businesses are willing to pay higher rents to live or operate closer to the central business district (CBD), while lower-income groups or less profitable land uses (like residential housing) move outward to cheaper areas. This explains why **wealthier families** tend to reside in prime, high-rent locations near the city center. The other options either misrepresent the theory or are unrelated to its core concept.
